name: Small Bird and Bacon Stew with Walnuts or Hazelnuts
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces hazelnuts or walnuts, rough chop, roasted
salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Use 6 birds if they are really small.
2. Fry the bacon with the garlic until it is lightly browned in a heavy bottomed casserole.
3. Add the birds and brown on all sides.
4. Add the mushrooms and nuts, continue to cook for a couple of minutes.
5. Add the ale and water with the bay leaves.
6. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer very gently for 2 - 2 1/2 hours until the birds are falling off the bone.
7. Remove the birds from the juices and cool completely.
8. Remove any excess fat.
9. Serve the birds whole or carved on the bone, returning them to the juices and reheating gently if desired.
10. Adjust the seasoning to taste and serve with slices of bread and plenty of juices and bits.
11. A good green salad is an excellent accompaniment.
